title: test/e2e: add aardvark specific tests
body:
Signed-off-by: Lokesh Mandvekar <lsm5@fedoraproject.org>

<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

In case you're only changing docs, make sure to prefix the pull-request title with "[CI:DOCS]".  That will prevent functional tests from running and save time and energy.

Finally, be sure to sign commits with your real name.  Since by opening
a PR you already have commits, you can add signatures if needed with
something like `git commit -s --amend`.
-->

comments.body: @baude can you check the `Docker-py Compat` test. Nothing in the PR should affect the `alpine` image from what I can see.

Does this help?

```
diff --git a/test/e2e/run_aardvark_test.go b/test/e2e/run_aardvark_test.go
index f00c92aa4..0e125713d 100644
--- a/test/e2e/run_aardvark_test.go
+++ b/test/e2e/run_aardvark_test.go
@@ -39,7 +39,7 @@ var _ = Describe("Podman run networking", func() {
 
 	})
 
-	It("Aardvark Test 1: One container", func() {
+	FIt("Aardvark Test 1: One container", func() {
 		netName := createNetworkName("Test")
 		session := podmanTest.Podman([]string{"network", "create", netName})
 		session.WaitWithDefaultTimeout()
@@ -49,14 +49,13 @@ var _ = Describe("Podman run networking", func() {
 		ctrID := podmanTest.Podman([]string{"run", "-dt", "--name", "aone", "--network", netName, nginx})
 		ctrID.WaitWithDefaultTimeout()
 		cid := ctrID.OutputToString()
-		ip := podmanTest.Podman([]string{"exec", cid, "dig", "+short", "google.com"})
+		ip := podmanTest.Podman([]string{"exec", cid, "dig", "+short", "aone"})
 		ip.WaitWithDefaultTimeout()
 		Expect(ip.OutputToString()).To(MatchRegexp(IPRegex))
 		Expect(ip).Should(Exit(0))
-
 		ctrIP := podmanTest.Podman([]string{"inspect", "--format", fmt.Sprintf(`"{{.NetworkSettings.Networks.%s.IPAddress}}"`, netName), cid})
 		ctrIP.WaitWithDefaultTimeout()
-		cip := ctrIP.OutputToString()
+		cip := strings.ReplaceAll(ctrIP.OutputToString(), "\"", "")
 		Expect(cip).To(MatchRegexp(IPRegex))
 		Expect(ip).Should(Exit(0))
 
@@ -66,7 +65,7 @@ var _ = Describe("Podman run networking", func() {
 		revListArray := reverseLookup.OutputToStringArray()
 		Expect(revListArray).Should(HaveLen(2))
 		Expect(strings.TrimRight(revListArray[0], ".")).To(Equal("aone"))
-		Expect(strings.TrimRight(revListArray[1], ".")).To(Equal(cid[12]))
+		Expect(strings.TrimRight(revListArray[1], ".")).To(Equal(cid[:12]))
 
 	})
 
@@ -124,7 +123,7 @@ var _ = Describe("Podman run networking", func() {
 		})
 	*/
 
-	FIt("Aardvark Test 4: Two containers, different subnets", func() {
+	It("Aardvark Test 4: Two containers, different subnets", func() {
 		netNameA := createNetworkName("TestA")
 		sessionA := podmanTest.Podman([]string{"network", "create", netNameA})
 		sessionA.WaitWithDefaultTimeout()
@@ -168,7 +167,7 @@ var _ = Describe("Podman run networking", func() {
 		Expect(resB1A1.OutputToString()).To(Equal(""))
 	})
 
-	FIt("Aardvark Test 5: Two containers on their own subnets, one container on both", func() {
+	It("Aardvark Test 5: Two containers on their own subnets, one container on both", func() {
 		netNameA := createNetworkName("TestA")
 		sessionA := podmanTest.Podman([]string{"network", "create", netNameA})
 		sessionA.WaitWithDefaultTimeout()
```

Test 2 flakes like so:

```
 Failure [3.752 seconds]
Podman run networking
/home/lsm5/go/src/github.com/containers/podman/test/e2e/run_aardvark_test.go:14
  Aardvark Test 2: Two containers, same subnet [It]
  /home/lsm5/go/src/github.com/containers/podman/test/e2e/run_aardvark_test.go:73

  Expected
      <[]string | len:0, cap:0>: nil
  to have length 2

  /home/lsm5/go/src/github.com/containers/podman/test/e2e/run_aardvark_test.go:128
```

Test 1 flakes similarly:
``` 
Failure [2.869 seconds]
Podman run networking
/home/lsm5/go/src/github.com/containers/podman/test/e2e/run_aardvark_test.go:14
  Aardvark Test 1: One container [It]
  /home/lsm5/go/src/github.com/containers/podman/test/e2e/run_aardvark_test.go:42

  Expected
      <[]string | len:0, cap:0>: nil
  to have length 2

  /home/lsm5/go/src/github.com/containers/podman/test/e2e/run_aardvark_test.go:67
```
@baude @flouthoc @Luap99 @mheon ready for review. Tests 1 and 2 flake sometimes (1 in 3 chance or less per current observations) which I'll debug first thing tomorrow.

added some sleeps and ran the current version across 20 nodes with 20 flakeAttempts, and 0 flakes so far.

PTAL all.

> added some sleeps and ran the current version across 20 nodes with 20 flakeAttempts, and 0 flakes so far.
> 
> PTAL all.

Im not super pleased to use timeouts long-term.  I'll look at where you used them and see if I can find a root cause.

@Luap99 if you have ideas, i'd love to hear them.

Yeah I think the timeouts are to long, the best way to handle it when timeouts are required is to try the test and if it fails wait 1 second and try again. This way it it will not take so long but still works on slow CI systems.

I think with dns we always need some special timeouts or have flakes, netavark doesn't wait for aardvark to update so there is not much we can do. Since this is just a SIGHUB I don't see us fixing this without changing the way netavark calls aardvark,
if we only need to care about the case of 3 nodes and 3 flakeAttempts, I can probably reduce the sleeps by quite a bit and investigate further.

If we don't want sleep one generic way could be to create a function in `podman` which verifies committed aardvark entries and the perform dns operation. Cause `sighup` is almost immediate as soon as we write entries.
LGTM and happy green test buttons
@baude @Luap99 @flouthoc PTAL. still flakes on rare runs though.
@lsm5 LGTM. If hope flakes are extremely rare, we can increase retry limit by a few number if this ever becomes a problem.
